bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid. The opponent’s opening offer gives me only moderate utility, and with no messages or concession pattern yet, I will anchor on my strongest values across the high-weight issues.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.02 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s second offer is actually worse for my utility than their first, so I do not read meaningful concession from them; I will only concede on low-weight issueD, where they have consistently proposed valueC.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.93 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers have not improved for me; in fact, their utility to me has declined from about 0.44 to 0.35. Since they have repeatedly offered issueD: valueC and issueB: valueB, I am only making a small concession on the low-weight issueD while holding firm on the most valuable issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 because it is still very early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers give me low utility without a clear concession trend. The opponent repeatedly favors issueB=valueB, issueC=valueC, and issueD=valueC, so I am only conceding on the low-weight issueD while holding firm on my high-weight priorities.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still early at t=0.08 and the opponent’s offers have not improved for me; their latest offer is actually worse for my utility than earlier ones. Since they appear to prefer issueD:valueC in several offers and issueD is my lowest-weight issue, I have already shown flexibility there, but for this round I will avoid repeating my previous bid and return to my strongest package.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still early in the negotiation at t=0.10, so there is little need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s offers have not shown a consistent concession trend and remain much worse for me than my bids, so I will only concede on the lowest-weight issueD while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a small concession on the low-weight issueD. The opponent’s offers remain low for my utility and do not show a clear concession pattern, so I am holding firm on my most important issues A, B, and C while varying my prior bid to avoid repeating exactly.

I am targeting a high-utility bid because it is still very early at t=0.14 and the opponent’s offers have not shown a reliable concession trend; their latest bid is actually quite low for me. I will keep firm on the high-weight issues A, B, and C, while using the low-weight issueD as the main concession area.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 while making only a low-cost concession. The opponent’s offers to me remain low and inconsistent, though their latest improved slightly on issueA; they also often choose issueD: valueD, so I concede there while holding firm on the high-weight issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid of about 0.961 because it is still early at t=0.18, so there is no need to concede on the three high-weight issues yet. The opponent has only slightly improved their offers for me and often uses issueD: valueC, which is low-weight for me, so I can accommodate that while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC.

At t=0.20 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 while making a small concession on the low-weight issueD. The opponent has repeatedly offered issueD:valueD and seems to prefer alternatives to my ideal there, but their offers still give me relatively low utility and show only limited concession, so I will hold firm on the three high-importance issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96 since it is still early at t=0.22 and there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent’s offers have improved somewhat for issueA but remain weak for my high-weight issues, especially issueB and issueC, so I will only concede on the low-weight issueD while holding my top values on the main issues.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92, conceding only on the low-weight issueD while holding firm on the three 0.3-weight issues. At t=0.24 it is still early, and the opponent’s offers remain low for me and show no reliable upward concession trend, though they often choose issueD: valueD, so I can trade that away first. No opponent messages were provided, so I rely only on the valid offer history.

At t=0.26 it is still relatively early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession to avoid simply repeating my last offer. The opponent’s valid offers have not improved much for me overall, though they have sometimes moved issueA to valueB; I will hold firm on the high-weight issues and vary only the low-weight issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.96, conceding only on low-weight issueD while holding firm on the three high-weight issues. At t=0.28 there is still no need for major concessions, though the opponent has improved somewhat by offering issueA:valueB and issueB:valueB in the latest bid. At t=0.30 I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.81 while making a visible concession beyond only varying issueD. The opponent has repeatedly moved toward issueA:valueB and seems to resist my ideal issueA:valueA, but they have not offered enough on issueB or issueC for me to concede those high-weight issues yet.

I am targeting a strong utility around 0.77 while making a meaningful concession on the low-weight issueD to avoid simply repeating my prior bid. At t=0.36 I am still targeting a fairly strong bid around 0.79 utility, since there is time left and the opponent has recently conceded on issueA and sometimes issueB. I targeted a utility around 0.79, keeping my strongest remaining demand on issueC while accepting the opponent’s apparent movement toward issueA=valueA and issueB=valueC. I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.75, conceding on lower-cost issueD while holding firm on high-value issueC. The opponent has gradually moved toward my preferences on issueA and issueB, and their latest offer suggests issueC remains the main gap; with time at 0.42, I should not concede my highest remaining priority yet. I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.79, maintaining my key high-value position on issueA and issueC while continuing to concede on issueB and the lower-weight issueD. At t=0.46 we are in the mid-game, so I am targeting a solid but not maximal utility bid, conceding mainly on lower-cost dimensions while holding issueA and issueC where my utility is high. The opponent has increasingly accepted issueA=valueA in their own offers, but continues pushing away from my preferred issueC=valueB, so I will keep issueC firm and make concessions on issueB/issueD instead. I am targeting a utility of about 0.81: still strong for me, but with a meaningful mid-game concession on issueC and issueD. The opponent has repeatedly pushed issueC:valueC and often offered issueD:valueC, while also showing some willingness to give issueA:valueA and issueB:valueA, so I will hold those high-value issues and concede where their bidding suggests importance.
